ksa_text: Develop and deliver data and AI literacy programs for organizational audiences, including curriculum and training material design, on-the-job training programs, delivery facilitation, and effectiveness assessment.

dcwf.top_element_label: Develop and document User Experience (UX) requirements including information architecture and user interface requirements.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of data remediation tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Knowledge of data remediation tools and techniques

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Included: task maps directly to Federated Governance Group Representative scope — GOV-specific signals detected ("data literacy", "ai literacy", "training   :program|material|delivery|curriculum|module"). STRM composite is strong (0.4584). Domain Operations & Program Management (affinity 0.65) is a natural fit for this governance role. Seniority is appropriate for manager-level level.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External check: no matches in governance job postings or certification curricula (CDMP, DAMA, IAPP CIPP/CIPM, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001). Would a working Federated Governance Group Representative self-report this as a required competency?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Challenge answered: the governance competency is implicit in Federated Governance Group Representative practice (domain affinity 0.65, 3 GOV signal(s)). Framework vocabulary matching underrepresents many legitimate governance competencies.
